description:
The pattern 'TemporaryDirectory → client.get(slot, path) → backup_copy(path, slot, name_hint)' is repeated 4 times:
- src/cli/cmd_slots.py:235-239 (cmd_rename)
- src/cli/cmd_audio.py:33-69 (cmd_optimize)
- src/cli/cmd_audio.py:129-142 (_optimize_all_process)
- src/cli/cmd_audio.py:314-317 (cmd_fingerprint)

Fix: Create download_slot_to_temp(client, slot, info, operation='op') → (temp_dir, wav_path) helper in src/core/ops.py. The helper creates the temp dir, downloads, and runs backup_copy. Callers get the wav_path for further processing.

Evidence: src/cli/cmd_slots.py:235-239, src/cli/cmd_audio.py:33-69, 129-142, 314-317
title: ARCH-014: Deduplicate download-and-backup pattern across CLI commands

description: When samples are optimized with speed != 1.0, the user needs to adjust pitch on the device to compensate. Two parts: (1) Mark/flag samples that were speed-adjusted so the user knows which ones need pitch correction. (2) Investigate whether we can set pitch automatically via metadata — check if sound.pitch or sound.rootnote in the upload metadata affects playback pitch on the device.
title: Mark speed-optimized samples and explore pitch metadata

description:
sys.path is manipulated in two separate places with different path calculations:
- krate.py:9-11 — Path(__file__).parent / 'src'
- src/tui/worker.py:20-24 — Path(__file__).parent.parent / 'src'

Both accomplish the same thing (add src/ to sys.path) but the duplication means:
1. If the project structure changes, both must be updated
2. worker.py's path calculation differs (parent.parent vs parent) due to its location in src/tui/
3. worker.py does it AFTER already importing from core (lines 17-18), making the ordering fragile

Fix: Either use a proper package install (setup.py/pyproject.toml with pip install -e .) or centralize the path setup in a single __init__ that all entry points use. The sys.path hack at line 20-24 of worker.py can be removed once proper packaging is in place.

Related: ko2-tools-jvr (release strategy) and META-002 (CLI rename) will likely address packaging.

Evidence: krate.py:9-11, src/tui/worker.py:20-24
title: ARCH-017: Consolidate sys.path manipulation to single entry point

description:
src/cli/ has 76 bare print() calls across 4 files, bypassing the View protocol:
- cmd_system.py: 25 violations (cmd_audit, cmd_fs_ls, status reporting)
- cmd_audio.py: 13 violations (cmd_optimize progress, optimize-all summary, fingerprint output)
- cmd_slots.py: 11 violations (squash reporting, move/copy progress, blank separators)
- display.py: 27 violations (TerminalView rendering — these may be intentional since TerminalView IS the stdout renderer, but should still go through a controlled output path)

These break --quiet and --json modes. All output should go through view.info()/view.step()/view.kv()/view.progress() methods.

Priority order: cmd_system.py (most violations, untestable), cmd_audio.py (user-facing progress), cmd_slots.py (operation feedback).

Note: Core layer prints are tracked separately in ko2-tools-kzh (ARCH-011).

Evidence: Grep for '^\s+print\(' across src/cli/ returns 76 matches.
title: ARCH-018: Route all 76 bare print() calls in src/cli/ through View protocol

description: Hardcoded values: 64MB memory assumption (ko2.py:455, app.py:541), 5KB savings threshold (ko2.py:802,931), audio2ko2 path (ko2.py:697). Fix: YAML/TOML config file. See ui.py:234 TODO. Effort: 1 day.
title: ARCH-006: Add configuration file support

description: Waveform precalc downloads run on the same worker thread. Even though the worker checks queue emptiness before starting a precalc step, once a download is in progress it blocks the thread. Any keypress that triggers a worker request (or state update) gets delayed. The rename input visibly lags. Fix: either pause waveform precalc while modals are open, or move precalc to a separate thread/process.
notes:
ADDITIONAL FINDINGS from tui-2026-03-21-210118.jsonl:

48 waveform downloads attempted. Total time: 13.6 minutes.
Download durations degrade over time: first downloads 1-4s, later ones 17s, 24s, 38s, 
62s, 98s, and one at 182s (3 minutes for single slot — 8047 GET_DATA pages, ~3.5MB sample).

Root causes:
1. Precalc downloads ALL slots including huge samples — no size limit or priority
2. Downloads not cancellable mid-transfer — blocks worker for minutes per large sample
3. No mechanism to interrupt precalc when user queues an operation
4. _initialize() called 14 times during large download window (hardcoded timeout overhead)

Fix priorities:
- P0: Make downloads cancellable (check queue between pages)
- P0: Skip precalc immediately when user request arrives
- P1: Size-limit precalc downloads (skip samples > N bytes)
- P1: Prioritize visible/selected slots over bulk precalc
title: TUI: waveform precalc blocks worker thread, user operations never execute

description: 400+ lines of argparse in main() at ko2.py:1618-2021. Move to ko2/parser.py. Effort: 4 hours.

description: SampleInfo, EP133Error, DeviceNotFoundError, and SlotEmptyError are domain types defined in src/core/client.py (transport layer). Anything needing the data type pulls in the mido-dependent transport module. Fix: move to src/core/models.py (or src/core/domain.py), add backward-compat aliases in client.py, update imports in cli/display.py, cmd_slots.py, cmd_transfer.py, tui/worker.py. See architect review 2026-03-19.
title: Move SampleInfo and exception types from client.py to models.py

close_reason: Already resolved — TUI has zero CLI imports after prior refactoring
description:
SampleFormat (src/cli/display.py:29-77) is a domain-level formatting utility imported by both CLI and TUI layers. src/tui/app.py:24 and src/tui/ui.py:15 import it from cli.display, creating a TUI→CLI dependency that violates the three-layer architecture (Core←CLI, Core←TUI). Combined with ko2-tools-6a8 (CLI→TUI via WaveformStore), this forms a bidirectional circular dependency between the two presentation layers.

Fix: Move SampleFormat to src/core/formatting.py (or src/core/display.py). Update imports in cli/display.py, tui/app.py, tui/ui.py. This is the highest-impact architectural fix — it and ko2-tools-6a8 together break the circular dependency.

Evidence: src/tui/app.py:24, src/tui/ui.py:15 both have 'from cli.display import SampleFormat'.
notes: BeeWare (Python) confirmed for mobile — all frontends are Python consumers of core/. SampleFormat is project-wide formatting, not CLI-specific. Move the whole class + _BANDS to core/formatting.py without splitting. No shared/ layer needed.
title: ARCH-010: Move SampleFormat to src/core/ to fix TUI→CLI layer violation

close_reason: All three arch P1 issues resolved in single refactoring session

The fingerprint command fails if textual is not installed. Fix: move to src/core/waveform_store.py and update imports in worker.py, app.py, cmd_audio.py, and test_tui_waveform_store.py. Pure mechanical rename, no behavior change. See architect review 2026-03-19.
title: Move WaveformStore to src/core/ to fix cli→tui coupling

description:
src/tui/debug_log.py:50-90 (DebugLogger) and src/tui/dialog_log.py:8-48 (DialogLogger) have near-identical structure:
- Identical __init__(): enabled flag, optional path, capture_dir, thread lock, file handle setup
- Identical close(): lock + file close
- Near-identical record(): enabled guard, lock, write+flush

Only the record() formatting differs (JSON events vs timestamped text lines).

Fix: Extract a BaseLogger class with shared __init__, close, and a _write(line: str) method. Subclass both to specialize only the record() formatting. ~30 lines of duplicated boilerplate eliminated.

Evidence: src/tui/debug_log.py:50-90, src/tui/dialog_log.py:8-48
title: ARCH-012: Deduplicate DebugLogger and DialogLogger into shared base class

description: probe_channels() sends DownloadInitRequest + one chunk then stops; device abandons after timeout. Currently safe (read-only audit loops only) but if ever called before delete()/put() could cause silent failure. Fix: add _initialize() at end, or add warning comment to all call sites. Effort: 30 min.
title: ARCH-009: Guard probe_channels() callers against partial-download state

description:
The pattern 'get node_id from entry → client.get_node_metadata() → extract name/channels/samplerate with fallbacks' is repeated 3 times:
- src/cli/cmd_slots.py:48-61 (_ls_scan_fs)
- src/cli/cmd_slots.py:180-193 (cmd_info)
- src/cli/cmd_system.py:215-228 (cmd_audit)

Each copy does the same None-safe extraction: node_meta.get('name'), node_meta.get('channels'), node_meta.get('samplerate') with int() conversions and fallbacks.

Fix: Extract a shared helper like enrich_sample_from_node(client, entry) → dict in src/cli/naming.py or a new src/cli/metadata.py. Returns enriched fields (name, channels, samplerate) or None.

Evidence: src/cli/cmd_slots.py:48-61, cmd_slots.py:180-193, src/cli/cmd_system.py:215-228
title: ARCH-013: Deduplicate metadata-fetch pattern across CLI commands

close_reason: All items already cleaned up in prior sessions. UPLOAD_END and CHANNELS are actually in use (debug_log.py and tests respectively).
description:
Several items are defined but never used or only used via unnecessary indirection:

1. SysExCmd.UPLOAD_END (0x6D) — models.py:29 — defined, never used in any request class
2. RendererProtocol = View — display.py:122 — backward-compat alias, never imported anywhere
3. TerminalRenderer = TerminalView — display.py:250 — backward-compat alias, never imported
4. CHANNELS constant — models.py:56 — exported in core/__init__.py, never used by any consumer
5. _detect_channels, _decode_samples aliases — audio.py:130,133 — backward-compat, never imported
6. worker.py:26-28 imports public names and re-aliases them to private names (confusing):
   extract_waveform_bins as _extract_waveform_bins_from_wav_bytes
   extract_fingerprint as _extract_fingerprint_from_wav_bytes

Fix: Remove items 1-5 outright. For item 6, use the public names directly in worker.py.

Note: BIT_DEPTH (models.py:55) IS used once at client.py:741-742, so keep it.

Evidence: Grep confirms no imports of RendererProtocol, TerminalRenderer, CHANNELS, _detect_channels, _decode_samples outside their definition files.
title: ARCH-015: Clean up dead code — unused constants, aliases, and exports

description:
Size-band color mapping is defined in two separate, disconnected palettes:
- CLI: TerminalView._ANSI_PALETTE in src/cli/display.py:129-136 (ANSI 256-color codes)
- TUI: _RICH_PALETTE in src/tui/ui.py:18-25 (Rich hex colors)

A comment in ui.py:17 says 'parallel to TerminalRenderer._ANSI_PALETTE' but there's no shared definition, no tests ensuring they stay aligned, and no mapping between the two.

Fix: Define size bands as a shared enum or constant in src/core/ (e.g., SizeBand with thresholds). Each presentation layer maps SizeBand → its own color format. This way band logic is defined once and colors can diverge per renderer without risk of threshold drift.

Also: SampleFormat.size_band() in display.py:65-77 computes bands — this logic should move with SampleFormat (see ko2-tools-ARCH-010).

Evidence: src/cli/display.py:129-136, src/tui/ui.py:17-25
title: ARCH-016: Unify color palette definitions between CLI and TUI

description: get() in src/core/client.py calls _initialize() only in the success path (line 629). If _download_data() raises (e.g. EP133Error 'Failed to get file info'), _initialize() is never called. Device stays in partial-download mode. Subsequent list_sounds() call in _emit_inventory's finally block gets silently dropped by device, returns {}, apply_inventory({}) marks all 999 slots as empty — all samples disappear from view. Happens ~30% of moves due to timing race with device auto-timeout. Fix: wrap download+save in get() with try/finally so _initialize() always fires. Same issue exists in waveform precalc path (_download_slot_wav_bytes -> client.get()).
title: Fix get() leaving device in partial-download mode on exception

close_reason: Download data RX (cmd 0x3D) now skips fileop decoding — labels as GET_DATA or GET_INIT_RSP instead of false PUT_INIT/DELETE/VERIFY
description: The TUI trace parser (app.py _format_trace_line) tries to decode every RX message as a protocol operation. When waveform precalc downloads audio data, the packed7-encoded PCM bytes get misinterpreted as PUT_INIT, DELETE, VERIFY etc. with garbage slot numbers (53230, 21085, etc.). This makes debug logs misleading and hard to analyze. Fix: skip or label RX messages that are download data responses (devid 0x3D, GET_DATA chunks).
title: Debug log misparses download data chunks as protocol operations

description: User reports that after move or upload in the TUI, the samples view sometimes shows all slots as empty. Cannot reproduce with CLI-style sequential calls (10/10 pass). Suspect: _initialize() in client.py fires without checking responses — if device is slow to process previous operation, init may silently fail, leaving device in bad state for subsequent list_sounds(). Potential fixes: (1) validate init responses, (2) add small delay after delete before list_sounds, (3) retry list_sounds on empty result. Waveform precalc and worker thread serialization ruled out as causes.
title: TUI: samples view shows all empty after move/upload (~30% repro)

description: Press Enter to load adds friction. Fix: option to auto-load on cursor movement with throttling. Trade-off: increased MIDI traffic. Effort: Medium.
title: TUI-010: Waveform auto-load option

description:
TUI state is scattered across multiple locations with no clear ownership model:

1. TuiState (src/tui/state.py) — slots dict + details_by_slot dict (dual tracking)
2. TUIApp.moving_src (src/tui/app.py:~153) — transient UI state on the app instance
3. TUIApp._waveform_by_slot / _waveform_pending (app.py) — cache state not synced with TuiState

Risks identified:
- After clear_slot(), loaded=True but slot is empty — UI may show stale data
- moving_src is never cleared on cancel/error, potentially leaving UI stuck in move-preview mode
- Multiple code paths can mutate slot metadata (apply_inventory, apply_slot_details, apply_inventory_updates, clear_slot) with no validation that mutations don't conflict
- Waveform cache can become stale when slots are modified without cache invalidation

Fix: Document which code paths own mutation of each state field. Consider making TuiState the single source of truth by moving waveform cache references into it. Add a state.clear_move() helper. Audit clear_slot() to ensure it properly resets all derived state.

Evidence: src/tui/state.py:40-110, src/tui/app.py:~146-160
title: TUI-015: Clarify TUI state ownership and mutation boundaries

close_reason: Resolved: all upload operations now use opcode 0x7E (UPLOAD), matching the official TE app. Confirmed from captures/sniffer-upload21.jsonl and verified on real device with roundtrip test. PROTOCOL.md updated. The device also accepts 0x6C but 0x7E is canonical.
description:
Code (models.py) uses SysExCmd.UPLOAD_DATA (0x6C) for all upload requests (init, chunk, end, verify). PROTOCOL.md line 38 documents 0x7E (UPLOAD) as the opcode for 'File upload (PUT), DELETE'. Line 126-129 acknowledges both: 'Command byte: 0x7E (or 0x6C / 0x7F)'.

The code works correctly (confirmed by captures), but the documentation is ambiguous about when to use which opcode. Delete uses 0x7E while upload uses 0x6C — this split is not clearly explained.

Additionally, SysExCmd.UPLOAD_END (0x6D) is defined at models.py:29 but never used. A comment at models.py:278 says 'Using 0x6D (UPLOAD_END) here was wrong.'

Action items:
1. Update PROTOCOL.md to clearly document that uploads use 0x6C, deletes use 0x7E
2. Add a rationale note explaining why (captured from official tool behavior)
3. Remove or mark UPLOAD_END (0x6D) as deprecated dead code
4. Cross-reference with captures that confirm 0x6C for uploads

Evidence: src/core/models.py:28-30 (opcode defs), models.py:255-295 (upload requests), PROTOCOL.md lines 38, 126-129
title: PROT-008: Reconcile upload opcode 0x6C vs 0x7E between code and PROTOCOL.md

description: Run: pytest tests/e2e/test_squash.py -v -m e2e. Verifies client.get() calling _initialize() after download fixes stale DELETE issue. Confirmed from tui-2026-03-03-042744.jsonl. Effort: 5 min (with hardware).
title: TEST-001: Verify squash/download fix on hardware

description: Used in optimize/move/copy but untested. Scope: backup file naming, backup directory creation, collision handling. Effort: 4 hours.

Effort: 30 min research, capture only if pattern isn't documented.
title: PROT-005: Complete Pad Mapping Groups B/C/D

description: Enable hot-swap, deduplication, prevent data loss. SampleDatabase class, ko2 sync command CLI+TUI, SQLite schema, duplicate detection, unlimited library beyond 999 slots. See state.py:21 TODO. Effort: 3-5 days.

description: Visible flicker, lost scroll position. _refresh_table() clears and rebuilds all 999 rows. Fix: use DataTable.add_row()/update_cell() for incremental updates. Effort: Medium.
title: TUI-009: Eliminate full table rebuild

description: Silent data loss risk. action_upload() directly queues request without checking if slot is occupied. Fix: check if target slot exists, show confirmation modal. Effort: 1 hour.
title: TUI-003: Upload overwrite warning

description: Cannot show accurate free memory; falls back to 64MB. Evidence: sniffer-slot22.jsonl event 59 shows free_space_in_bytes. Action: python midi_proxy.py --proxy --spoof --hunt any captures/sniffer-storage-stats.jsonl. Effort: 30 min.
title: PROT-004: Capture Memory/Storage Statistics

close_reason: All three arch P1 issues resolved in single refactoring session
description:
Core layer must not do presentation. Three bare print() calls violate this:
- client.py:240 — debug output: print(f'  TX: {hx}...')
- client.py:529,533 — upload progress: print(f'\r  Uploading... {pct}%')
- client.py:690 — warning: print(f'  ⚠ Page mismatch...')

These prevent the core layer from being used headlessly or with alternative UIs. Fix: replace with callbacks (progress_callback for upload progress) or Python logging module. The TUI already has its own progress eventing via WorkerEvent; CLI commands should pass a progress callback from the View protocol.

Related: ko2-tools-2s1 (bare prints in CLI layer) addresses the CLI side; this issue is specifically about the core/protocol layer.
title: ARCH-011: Remove print() statements from src/core/client.py

description: When running with --debug, protocol trace messages (upload start, download ready, verify, etc.) flood the log pane, hiding actual user-facing events (OK, Error, Timing). Debug trace should only go to the JSONL capture file, not the in-app log view. The log pane should show only: inventory refreshed, operation results (OK/Error), timing summaries.
title: TUI: debug trace messages should not appear in log pane

description: src/tui/worker.py has a 93-line if/elif chain dispatching on req.op string values. Adding a new operation requires touching actions.py, worker.py, and app.py with no compile-time check. Fix: replace with _OP_HANDLERS: dict[str, Callable] registry built at class definition time. Makes supported operations discoverable, eliminates silent fall-through risk, reduces boilerplate for future ops. No user-visible behavior change. See architect review 2026-03-19.
title: Replace worker.py if/elif dispatch chain with _OP_HANDLERS registry

close_reason: stale: history cleanup done, repo is publish-ready
description: Prepare for open source release: clean git history, reorganize repo (separate protocol spec, main README), package as installable app, Homebrew tap, semantic versioning. Effort: 2-3 days.
title: META-001: Git history cleanup and repo prep for publishing

close_reason: ko2.py has been split into src/cli/ module commands and tests have been updated appropriately
description: 2072 lines in single file; functional but hard to navigate. Target: ko2/cli.py, ko2/commands/{ls,transfer,optimize,squash}.py, ko2/parser.py, ko2/helpers.py. Effort: 2-3 days.
title: ARCH-001: Split ko2.py into modules

description: client.py _initialize() uses hardcoded timeouts (0.2s, 0.6s) for recv_sysex calls and doesn't validate responses. This is fragile — if the device is slow (still processing a previous operation), init silently fails. The subsequent operations then get garbage or no response. This is the likely root cause of the TUI showing all-empty after move/upload (~30% repro). Fix: wait for expected responses with proper matching, not blind timeouts.
title: Remove hardcoded timeouts from _initialize()

description: Multi-slot selection hard to see. Fix: highlight selected rows with background color, show selection summary in panel/overlay, add select-all-filled shortcut. Effort: Medium.
title: TUI-011: Selection feedback enhancement

description:
The official TE Sample Tool draws waveforms when a pad is selected, but it's
unclear if it performs a full sample download to do so. Our TUI downloads the
entire sample via GET protocol to extract waveform bins — this is slow and
blocks the worker thread (see ko2-tools-36z).

Possible alternatives the official app might use:
- A dedicated waveform/thumbnail protocol command we haven't discovered
- Partial download (first N pages only)
- Cached from a previous full download
- Computed on-device and sent as metadata

Investigation: capture official app traffic when selecting a pad with a sample,
look for any protocol messages that aren't full GET downloads. Compare traffic
volume vs sample size.
title: Investigate how official TE app renders waveforms without full download

description: Replicate ~/.justx/koss.sh: convert any audio file to mono at 44100 Hz, preserving bit depth (16-bit -> pcm_s16le, 24-bit -> pcm_s24le). Output defaults to {input}_mono.wav. Uses ffmpeg. Add as CLI subcommand (krate lobotomize <input> [output]) and as a TUI action on selected slot(s).
title: CLI+TUI: lobotomize command — convert audio to mono preserving bit depth

description: Swap preview is subtle. Fix: distinct highlight for source slot, show 'Move: 043 -> 087' in floating overlay, animate affected rows. Effort: Medium.
title: TUI-012: Move mode visual clarity
META-002: Rename CLI entry point ko2 → krate

description: Tests use hardcoded values. Targets: test_wire_robustness.py, test_cli_output.py error cases. Fix: use @pytest.mark.parametrize. Effort: 4 hours.
title: TEST-006: Parametrize existing tests

close_reason: File picker now uses larger popup (90%/85%, max 120x50), fade-in animation, selection markers in tree
description: File picker (yazi) currently takes over the entire terminal via app.suspend(). This is jarring. Should be a large popup/overlay within the TUI, with a smooth transition animation. Consider using Textual's built-in DirectoryTree widget or a custom modal instead of shelling out to yazi.
title: TUI: file picker should be a popup overlay, not fullscreen

description: Set up: required status checks (tests must pass), branch protection on main (no force push, require PRs), PR template with checklist (protocol claims cited, tests added), CODEOWNERS if needed.
title: PR infra and branch protection rules

description: No Textual pilot tests for key interactions. Risk of regressions in keybinds, modal flows. Fix: test_tui_keybinds.py using Textual app.press() API. Effort: 1 day.
title: TEST-005: Add TUI widget tests

close_reason: beads backup exported to git, branch pushed to origin
description: Once the GitHub remote is configured, run bd backup export-git to publish issues to the beads-backup branch. Consider hooking into pre-push so it runs automatically. Also document bd backup fetch-git in CONTRIBUTING.md for new contributors.
title: Set up bd backup export-git after GitHub repo creation

close_reason: Confirmed BE16 from device capture (430 entries, 214 divergent — all matched BE16). Removed U14 heuristic, decode_node_id is now a one-liner.
description:
models.py decode_node_id() tries both BE16 (hi<<8|lo) and U14 (hi<<7|lo) with a filename-prefix heuristic. This is undocumented and the actual device encoding is unverified.

Done:
- Documented LIST response format and the encoding question in PROTOCOL.md
- Added diagnostic log when BE16 and U14 interpretations diverge

Still needed:
- Capture LIST response from a device with samples in slots 128+ (where BE16 and U14 give different results)
- Compare raw packed7-decoded bytes against known slot numbers
- Once encoding is confirmed, simplify decode_node_id() to use the correct one
title: PROT-007: Document node_id dual-decode heuristic in PROTOCOL.md

description: No discoverable keybinding help. Footer shows only active bindings in one hard-to-scan line. Fix: add ? or h key to display full keybinding reference modal as vertical list/table. Effort: 2-3 hours.
title: TUI-002: Keyboard help overlay

description:
Observed in capture tui-2026-03-21-210118.jsonl:

Downloads 1-31: 230-400 pages/s
Download 32 (during/after file picker): 113 pages/s  
Downloads 33-47: 24-37 pages/s

Throughput dropped 10x and did not recover for the rest of the session.
The drop coincides with the yazi file picker (app.suspend/resume) but
causation is not established — could be device state, MIDI driver,
accumulated protocol errors, or something else.

Needs investigation: reproduce with and without file picker to isolate cause.
title: TUI: MIDI throughput drops 10x mid-session (observed after file picker)
